App.name: App
App.description:
Public face of an app installed in the desktop.

Apps are the central registry: a single object describing one of these is
picked up by the dock, the menu bar, Spotlight, keyboard shortcuts, and any
other surface that knows how to look at the app list. Contributing to one
place contributes to all of them.

fields.name: id
fields.description: URL- and css-safe id. Used as the stable window id (`app:<id>`).

fields.name: name
fields.description: Human-readable name shown in the dock, menu bar, and Spotlight.

fields.name: tagline
fields.description: Optional one-line subtitle for Spotlight and dock tooltips.

fields.name: accent
fields.description:
Accent color. Themes use it to tint the dock tile gradient, the window
top-edge highlight, and the menu-bar status dot when this app is focused.
When absent, the theme's default accent is used.

fields.name: icon
fields.description: Lucide-style icon. Themes render a fallback letter when absent.

fields.name: iconArt
fields.description:
Subject illustration painted inside the dock tile, on top of the accent
gradient. Optional; themes fall back to `icon` or a letter.

fields.name: defaultBounds
fields.description: Default window bounds when first opened. Theme has the fallback.

fields.name: content
fields.description: The window's body.

fields.name: focused
fields.description: True if this window is the currently focused one.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Theme id for storage namespacing.

fields.name: name
fields.description: Display name shown in dev tooling.

fields.name: customizable
fields.description:
End-user tweakable subset of the theme. Keys are dotted paths into the
theme object (e.g. `"palette.accent"`, `"shape.dockTileRadius"`). The
Settings system app reads this map and renders one field per entry; the
library persists the chosen value via the storage adapter and overlays
it on top of the theme defaults to produce the effective theme.

A theme that omits this field exposes no settings panel. A theme that
supplies ten entries gets a ten-field panel for free.

fields.name: background
fields.description: Fallback background when no wallpaper is set.

fields.name: surface
fields.description: Surface tint applied to menu bar, dock, and window chrome.

fields.name: textPrimary
fields.description: Primary text color on surfaces.

fields.name: textSecondary
fields.description: Secondary / muted text.

fields.name: accent
fields.description: Default accent. Apps may override per-app.

fields.name: border
fields.description: Border / hairline color.

fields.name: small
fields.description: Small radius for pills, tooltips, menu-bar chips.

fields.name: missionControlDurationMs
fields.description:
Mission Control spread and collapse. Its own gesture, distinct from the
window open and genie pacing (the macOS expose zoom runs roughly 0.2 to
0.25s).

fields.name: surface
fields.description: Backdrop-filter applied to surface chrome (menu bar, dock).

fields.name: spotlight
fields.description: Backdrop-filter applied to Spotlight.

fields.name: src
fields.description: Wallpaper image url. When absent, the palette background fills the desktop.

fields.name: parallax
fields.description: Enables cursor-driven parallax.

fields.name: vignette
fields.description: Adds a dark vignette over the wallpaper.

fields.name: options
fields.description: Hex / CSS color strings the user picks from.

fields.name: unit
fields.description: Optional unit suffix shown next to the numeric readout.

StorageAdapter.name: StorageAdapter
StorageAdapter.description:
Abstract storage backend. The library ships a localStorage adapter and
accepts custom ones for products that want server-side persistence or
cross-device sync.

fields.name: subscribe
fields.description:
Notify a listener whenever a stored value changes. Returns an unsubscribe
function. Listeners receive the unprefixed key.

SpotlightResult.name: SpotlightResult
SpotlightResult.description:
One result row contributed by a Spotlight source. Sources are how features
outside the apps registry and the system-window registry surface in the
Cmd-K palette: recently opened docs pages, presets, downloads, bookmarks,
or anything else the consumer wants to make findable.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Stable id used as the React key and the dedup token.

fields.name: name
fields.description: Visible row label.

fields.name: tagline
fields.description: Optional one-line subtitle on the right.

fields.name: accent
fields.description: Accent color tinting the row's tile gradient.

fields.name: icon
fields.description: Optional icon node rendered inside the tile.

fields.name: kindLabel
fields.description: Source-side label such as "Doc · Spotlight", "Preset · Tubes".

fields.name: onActivate
fields.description: What to do when the row is activated (Enter or click).

SpotlightSource.name: SpotlightSource
SpotlightSource.description:
A Spotlight source returns the current set of results for a given query.
Receives the raw query string (lowercased, trimmed) and returns the rows
to merge into the Spotlight panel. Returning an empty array when the
query doesn't apply is the convention.
